answer:
\boxed{George Steiner}

Steiner’s *Real Presences* (1989) critiques Hegel’s influence on modern philosophy, arguing that Hegel’s dialectical method undermined traditional metaphysics and contributed to philosophy’s shift toward literary and interpretive forms. He contrasts this with Nietzsche’s and Heidegger’s existential self-creation, emphasizing Proust’s unique literary engagement with time and subjectivity. This work remains a key reference in discussions of philosophy’s post-metaphysical turn.
